references.title: Predicting academic performance of students from VLE big data using deep learning models

extracted.Features: [highest_education, imd_band, age_band, num_of_prev_attempts, studied_credits, disability, AC T. Clicks, BC T. Clicks, AC Dataplus, AC DualPane, AC Folder, AC Forumng, AC HomePage, AC OuCollaborate, AC OUcontent, AC OUwiki, AC Page, AC Questionnaire, AC Quiz, AC RepeatActivity, AC Resource, AC SubPage, AC Url, Tc_Activity, PreA-1, BC Homepage, OnAsClicks, ModuleAsigns, LateAsignsSub, PostA-1, BC OUcontent, BC Subpage, AC External Quiz, BC Forumng, BC Url, AC OuIluminate, BC Page, BC OUwiki, BC Quiz, AC  Glossary, BC HtmlActivity, AC HtmlActivity, BC OUIluminate, BC Glossary, gender, BC External Quiz, BC Questionaire, AC SharedSubPage, BC DataPlus, region, BC SharedSubPage, BC Resources, BC Dualpane]
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Withdrawn students were removed from the dataset and Distinction was merged with Pass students to perform a binary classifcation task.

In total there are 54 features.
extracted.Performance Metric: Best Model: {Accuracy: 0.8448 - MLP, Precision: 0.86 - MLP, Recall: 0.81 - Logistic Regression}
extracted.Comments:
There are 2 analysis being performed. 

One using clickstream and data from all sources. The second using the quarterly clickstream.

Performance of the models tends to increase significantly as the semester advances - especially when it comes to the Pass-Fail and Pass-Withdrawn distinctions.
extracted.Limitations:
Quarterly temporal resolution.

No use of sequential data.

Class imbalance for some of the classification tasks.

Small number of Algorithms tested.

annotations.text: VLEs have changed the paradigm of analytics for educational purposes as now more data than ever is available. This works deploys Deep Learning on a set of unique features to predict students at risk.

Model achieves more than 90% accuracy, greatly outperforming Logistic Regression or SVM models. Including Legacy Data AND Assessment-related data greatly impacts the performance of a model. Also, students who go back to check previous lectures also seem to perform better.
